page: Amazon Web Services m7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the m7i.metal-24xl demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The server achieved top-tier performance in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, ray tracing, and text processing tests. Its memory subsystem delivers top-tier read-only bandwidth for cached small blocks and strong performance for uncached large-block reads and writes. Cryptographic operations and extended instructions also rank in the top tier, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA extensions. For database workloads, the server shows strong performance in Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests.

As a bare-metal instance with dedicated resources, the m7i.metal-24xl offers high resource density and eliminates virtualization overhead. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring direct hardware access and consistent performance without hypervisor interference. The server is well-suited for large-scale enterprise applications, software compilation pipelines, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference, where its AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets can be fully utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processors. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations and CPU-based machine learning inference, aided by built-in Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 support. This bare-metal configuration is highly suited for enterprise applications, database workloads, and intensive CPU-bound tasks requiring direct hardware access without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for enterprise and database workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise and database workloads.

384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, ray tracing, and software compilation
